mission.title: Install and Configure SurrealDB
mission.description: SurrealDB is a scalable, distributed, collaborative, document-graph database designed for the real-time web. It provides a flexible data model and is suitable for applications requiring high performance and scalability. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and management compared to traditional databases.

steps.title: Add the SurrealDB Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official SurrealDB Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add surrealdb https://charts.surrealdb.com

helm repo update

```

> This is the official repository for SurrealDB.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install SurrealDB with Helm
steps.description:
This command installs SurrealDB in the specified namespace with the desired version.

```bash

kubectl create namespace surrealdb

helm install surrealdb surrealdb/surrealdb --namespace surrealdb --version 3.0.2

```

> Creates a namespace for SurrealDB.
> Installs SurrealDB in the surrealdb namespace.

steps.title: Create an example configuration
steps.description:
This step creates a sample configuration for SurrealDB to demonstrate its capabilities.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: surrealdb.com/v1
kind: SurrealDB
metadata:
  name: example-db
  namespace: surrealdb
spec:
  database: example
EOF

```

> Creates a sample database configuration.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: surrealdb.com/v1
kind: SurrealDB
metadata:
  name: example-db
  namespace: surrealdb
spec:
  database: example
EOF

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the SurrealDB pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n surrealdb

```

> Lists all pods in the surrealdb namespace to verify they are running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall SurrealDB
uninstall.description:
This step removes SurrealDB from your cluster. Be aware that this will delete all data associated with the database.

```bash

helm uninstall surrealdb --namespace surrealdb

kubectl delete namespace surrealdb

```

> Uninstalls SurrealDB from the specified namespace.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade SurrealDB
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your data. This step will upgrade SurrealDB to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl exec -it <surrealdb-pod-name> -- surrealdb backup --output /backup

helm upgrade surrealdb surrealdb/surrealdb --namespace surrealdb --version 3.0.2

kubectl get pods -n surrealdb

```

> Backup your current database.
> Upgrades SurrealDB to the specified version.
> Verify that the pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to database'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the database service is not running or misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the pod status with 'kubectl get pods -n surrealdb' and ensure the service is correctly configured.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources to start pod'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for SurrealDB.

**Fix:**
Increase the resource limits in the Helm chart values and redeploy.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Database not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified database may not exist or was not created correctly.

**Fix:**
Verify the database configuration and ensure it was applied successfully.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Permission denied'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the RBAC settings are not correctly configured.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the service account has the necessary permissions to access the resources.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced new features for improved data handling and performance optimizations.
versionNotes.deprecations: No deprecations noted in this version.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the SurrealDB pods running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n surrealdb'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary resources.
